SAIL Recruitment

Join SAIL

We welcome enquiries from researchers and students who are excited about computer architecture and systems for data-intensive applications, such as AI and bioinformatics applications.

SAIL enquiries help us assess fit. Formal applications are submitted through King's College London.

Make an enquiry Apply

Important: Submitting the SAIL form is not a university application.

How joining works

1

Enquire with SAIL

Share your background, interests, and evidence of strong research alignment.

2

We review and respond

We review enquiries promptly and respond when your profile aligns well.

3

Apply through KCL

If invited, complete the formal KCL application to move forward.

Paths to join SAIL

PhD

Enquire first so we can check research alignment and supervision capacity.

  • Data-centric architecture, PIM/ISP, AI systems, memory/storage reliability
Enquire (SAIL form) Apply

Funded studentships

Occasional funded calls open during the year.

  • Availability changes by scheme and cycle
More info

Postdocs and fellowships

We welcome strong fellowship ideas aligned with SAIL priorities.

  • Share a concise proposal, timeline, and potential funding scheme
Enquire (SAIL form)

KCL MSc/BSc projects

For current KCL students seeking research projects or dissertation supervision.

  • Include modules taken, coding experience, and preferred topic
Enquire (SAIL form)

Visiting students and scholars

We can host short-term visits where there is clear research fit and confirmed funding.

  • Typical duration 3 to 12 months
Enquire (SAIL form)

Research focus and candidate fit

Topics we are currently exploring

  • Data-centric computer architecture and systems
  • Processing-in-Memory (PIM) and In-Storage Processing (ISP)
  • Software-hardware co-design for AI and bioinformatics
  • Reliable and secure memory and storage systems

What strengthens an enquiry

  • Strong fundamentals in architecture, systems, or related fields
  • Hands-on programming in C/C++ and/or Python
  • Specific research interests rather than broad topic labels
  • Evidence of research potential: projects, papers, artifacts, or open-source work

Frequently asked questions

Do I need to apply on KCL as well?

Yes. The SAIL form is for enquiries and fit checks. Formal admissions decisions only happen through the official KCL application process.

What should I include in my enquiry?

Share your CV, your current stage (degree or role), two to three research interests, relevant project links, and why SAIL is a good fit.

Can you review my CV before I apply?

Yes, we can give brief fit-oriented feedback when capacity allows, but this does not replace the KCL admissions review.

What funding routes are possible?

Funding can include self-funded study, scholarships, and occasional funded calls. Eligibility depends on the scheme and your profile.

How long does it take to hear back?

Enquiries are reviewed on a rolling basis. Response time varies with volume, but we aim to reply as promptly as possible for strong matches.
